title: Forecast and Risk
subtitle: How prepared evidence becomes forecast confidence and risk classification

nodes.label: Historical Window
nodes.definition: The selected lookback period used to learn usage behavior.
nodes.whyItMatters: Window choice influences trend stability and relevance.
nodes.safeSentence: We choose a practical history window that represents recent operating behavior.

nodes.label: Holdout Testing
nodes.definition: Testing forecast behavior on data excluded from setup calibration.
nodes.whyItMatters: Holdout checks whether forecasts generalize beyond fitting data.
nodes.safeSentence: Holdout testing gives a realistic check on forecast usefulness.

nodes.label: Forecast Horizon
nodes.definition: The future time span covered by the capacity projection.
nodes.whyItMatters: Horizon length must match decision lead times.
nodes.safeSentence: Forecast horizon is set to support operational planning windows.

nodes.label: Forecast vs Actual
nodes.definition: Comparison of projected values against observed usage outcomes.
nodes.whyItMatters: Performance checks bound confidence and expected error.
nodes.safeSentence: Forecast-versus-actual comparisons calibrate how much trust to place in projections.

nodes.label: Threshold Crossing
nodes.definition: A forecasted point where usage is expected to exceed configured limits.
nodes.whyItMatters: Crossing expectations drive urgency and action sequencing.
nodes.safeSentence: Threshold crossing projections indicate when intervention may be needed.

nodes.label: Headroom Burn-down
nodes.definition: Projected reduction of remaining safe capacity over time.
nodes.whyItMatters: Burn-down rate helps prioritize near-term planning decisions.
nodes.safeSentence: Headroom burn-down shows how quickly our capacity buffer is shrinking.

nodes.label: Confidence Check
nodes.definition: A practical reliability review combining error behavior and data quality context.
nodes.whyItMatters: Confidence checks prevent false precision in decision conversations.
nodes.safeSentence: Confidence checks ensure we present forecast output with appropriate caution.

nodes.label: SME Review
nodes.definition: Domain expert review of forecast assumptions and operational plausibility.
nodes.whyItMatters: SME context catches blind spots not visible in metrics alone.
nodes.safeSentence: SME review complements metrics with practical service knowledge.

nodes.label: Low Risk
nodes.definition: Risk band where projected usage remains comfortably below thresholds.
nodes.whyItMatters: Low-risk classification supports routine monitoring cadence.
nodes.safeSentence: Low risk indicates no immediate capacity intervention is required.

nodes.label: Medium Risk
nodes.definition: Risk band where projected usage approaches limits within planning horizon.
nodes.whyItMatters: Medium risk prompts preparatory actions before urgency escalates.
nodes.safeSentence: Medium risk signals that contingency planning should begin now.

nodes.label: High Risk
nodes.definition: Risk band where projected usage is near or above operational thresholds.
nodes.whyItMatters: High risk requires priority action and governance visibility.
nodes.safeSentence: High risk indicates urgent action to prevent service impact.

nodes.label: Breach Flag
nodes.definition: An explicit marker indicating probable or imminent threshold breach.
nodes.whyItMatters: Breach flags accelerate escalation and action ownership.
nodes.safeSentence: A breach flag moves the case into immediate response handling.

paths.label: Forecast Evidence to Risk Band
paths.description: Move from setup and holdout checks into practical risk classification.
paths.rehearsalPrompt: Explain how forecast validation influences risk classification and escalation.
